---
title: "Access Data"
date: today
---

## Overview

Approved files are distributed through **datalibweb**, in two collections (servers):

| Collection | Content |
|---|---|
| `FDPRAW` | Raw survey microdata, questionnaires and documentation |
| `FDP` | Harmonized microdata (50by35 schema) and harmonization programs |

The harmonized welfare module in the `FDP` collection is called **`WELF`**.

Two clients are available: the [`datalibweb` Stata package](https://github.com/worldbank/datalibweb) and the [`dlw` R package](https://github.com/worldbank/dlw). Both require the World Bank intranet/VPN, permission to access the collections, and the same datalibweb token used by PRIMUS (valid 30 days, renewed on the datalibweb website).

## File naming conventions

datalibweb survey identifiers guarantee replicability: versions are **never replaced** — new releases get a new version number, and previous vintages remain available. Always record the full survey ID used in an analysis.

**Raw (master) data** in `FDPRAW`:

```
CCC_YYYY_SSSS_V##_M
```

- `CCC` — 3-letter ISO/WDI country code (e.g. `COL`)
- `YYYY` — year data collection started
- `SSSS` — survey acronym (e.g. `GEIH`, `EHCVM`, `RHS`)
- `V##_M` — version of the **master** (original) data; first release is `V01`

**Harmonized data** in `FDP`:

```
CCC_YYYY_SSSS_V##_M_V##_A_FDP
```

- `V##_A` — version of the harmonization (**adaptation**), independent of the master version
- `FDP` — the collection of harmonized surveys

**Module files** append the module name to the survey ID. The 50by35 harmonized welfare file (V01) for Colombia is:

```
COL_2023_GEIH_V01_M_V01_A_FDP_WELF.dta
```

## Checking the catalog

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```r
library(dlw)

# all collections (servers) you can access
dlw_server_catalog()

# what is in a collection
dlw_server_catalog("FDPRAW")
dlw_server_catalog("FDP")

# everything available for one country, across collections
dlw_country_catalog("COL")

# files for one country within a collection
dlw_server_inventory("COL", server = "FDP")
```

## Stata

```stata
* interactive browser: click through region -> country -> collection
datalibweb

* list what is available for a country-year in a collection; when raw
* data is requested, datalibweb lists the individual files to pick from
datalibweb, country(COL) years(2023) type(FDPRAW) clear
```

:::

## Loading data

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```r
library(dlw)

# harmonized 50by35 welfare module (WELF) from the FDP collection
df <- dlw_get_data(
  country_code = "COL",
  filename     = "COL_2023_GEIH_V01_M_V01_A_FDP_WELF.dta",
  server       = "FDP"
)

# a raw file from the FDPRAW collection; year and survey (acronym) disambiguate
# the specific survey vintage — the R equivalent of Stata's surveyid()
raw <- dlw_get_data(
  country_code = "COL",
  year         = 2023,
  survey       = "GEIH",
  filename     = "individual_data_2023.dta",
  server       = "FDPRAW"
)
```

## Stata

```stata
* harmonized 50by35 welfare module (WELF) from the FDP collection;
* the latest master and harmonization versions are loaded by default
datalibweb, country(COL) years(2023) type(FDP) mod(WELF) clear

* pin specific vintages for replicability
datalibweb, country(COL) years(2023) type(FDP) mod(WELF) ///
    vermast(01) veralt(01) clear

* a raw file from the FDPRAW collection
datalibweb, country(COL) years(2023) type(FDPRAW) ///
    surveyid(COL_2023_GEIH_V01_M) filename(individual_data_2023.dta) clear

* several countries at once, appended
datalibweb, country(COL TCD UGA) years(2018 2022 2023) type(FDP) mod(WELF) clear
```

:::

## Authentication

Register the datalibweb token (from the datalibweb website) once every 30 days:

::: {.panel-tabset}

## R

```r
dlw_set_token("<your datalibweb token>")   # stored in the system keyring
```

## Stata

```stata
datalibweb, token(<your datalibweb token>) version(2)
```

:::

For citation guidance and further options (e.g. PPP conversion on load), see the `datalibweb` help file in Stata and the [datalibweb guidelines](https://github.com/worldbank/datalibweb); questions to the datalibweb team at `datalibweb@worldbank.org`.
